skill.name: organize-google-contacts
skill.description: Your Google Contacts — supercharged with notes, tags, and relationship memory via Rarefriend. Find anyone instantly, remember every conversation, and never lose context on a person again. Use when the user wants to: search people they know, find someone's email or phone, create or update a contact, add meeting notes or call summaries, tag contacts, bulk import a list of people, connect Google Contacts, or check what they know about someone. Triggers on: contact, contacts, Google Contacts, who do I know, find someone, person, people, notes about, meeting notes, tag, import contacts, network, what do I know about, who is, remember this about, add a note.
skill.summary: Manage Google Contacts with notes, tags, and relationship memory via the Rarefriend MCP integration.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: After evaluation, all 77 external_commands findings are false positives — backticks in documentation wrap function names in markdown code formatting, not shell execution. The 5 hardcoded URLs point to the legitimate Rarefriend service (rarefriend.com) and the skill's own GitHub repository. Hidden file references (e.g., ~/.cursor/mcp.json, ~/.config/Claude/) are standard MCP configuration paths. The skill is a legitimate Google Contacts management integration with no malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Shell-like syntax in documentation
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md and SETUP.md files contain backtick characters that the static scanner flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown code formatting wrapping function names like `list_connected_integrations`, not actual shell command execution. The only actual shell command is `claude mcp add` in the setup instructions, which is a documented MCP registration command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed all 77 flagged lines — all backticks are used for markdown inline code formatting around MCP tool names, not for command execution.

low_findings.title: External network calls to Rarefriend service
low_findings.description: The skill makes network calls to rarefriend.com, which is the legitimate service this skill integrates with. The skill requires OAuth credentials (RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_ID, RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_SECRET) configured by the user to connect to the Rarefriend MCP server.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are to the documented Rarefriend service endpoint and are required for the skill's core functionality. No suspicious external endpoints.

content.user_title: Organize Google Contacts with Notes and Tags
content.value_statement: Google Contacts only stores names and emails, losing all relationship context. This skill adds notes, tags, and search capabilities through the Rarefriend integration so you remember every conversation.
actual_capabilities: Search contacts by name, company, role, email, tag, or source
Create, update, and delete contacts through natural language commands
Add and search notes attached to any contact for meeting summaries or context
Tag contacts with custom labels and filter by tag
Bulk import lists of contacts (up to 50 per call)
Connect Google Contacts via OAuth and check sync status
limitations: Requires the Rarefriend MCP server to be configured before use
Bulk import is limited to 50 contacts per API call
Google Calendar and Outlook features require separate skill installations

use_cases.title: Find a contact quickly
use_cases.description: Ask who you know at a specific company or find a person by name, email, or tag without browsing your address book manually.

use_cases.title: Log meeting notes
use_cases.description: Create notes on a contact after every meeting or call to build a searchable history of interactions and context.

use_cases.title: Bulk import and tag contacts
use_cases.description: Import a list of new contacts from a spreadsheet or event and apply tags to categorize them by role, company, or relationship type.

prompt_templates.title: Search by company
prompt_templates.prompt: Who do I know at Stripe?
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding contacts at a specific company

prompt_templates.title: Add a contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new contact: Jane Smith, jane@example.com, VP Engineering at Anthropic.

prompt_templates.title: Log meeting notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a note to John Doe: Discussed potential partnership. Follow up next week with proposal.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk tag contacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Tag everyone at Sequoia as investor.

output_examples.input: Who do I know at Stripe?
output_examples.output: Found 3 contacts at Stripe: 1. Sarah Chen — VP Engineering (Google Contacts) 2. Mike Johnson — Product Manager (LinkedIn) 3. Alex Rivera — Designer (Added manually). Would you like details on any of them?

output_examples.input: Tag everyone at Sequoia as investor
output_examples.output: Found 5 contacts at Sequoia. Tagging all 5 as 'investor'. Done: Sarah Chen, James Wu, Priya Patel, David Kim, and Lisa Zhang are now tagged 'investor'.

output_examples.input: Add a note to John Doe: Discussed partnership, follow up next week
output_examples.output: Note created on John Doe: 'Discussed partnership, follow up next week'. Would you like to pin this note for visibility?

best_practices: Always call get_contact before update_contact to confirm current values with the user
Use searchMode: 'exact' when searching by full email or complete name for precise results
Mention the source attribution (Google Contacts, LinkedIn, Manual) when presenting search results
anti_patterns: Do not skip the get_contact confirmation step before updating contact fields
Do not attempt bulk imports exceeding 50 contacts in a single call
Do not search synced Google contacts before get_integration_sync_status returns complete

faq.question: How do I set up the skill?
faq.answer: Sign in at rarefriend.com, go to Settings, then Integrations, then MCP to get your Client ID and Secret. Run the claude mcp add command from the setup guide with those credentials.

faq.question: Does this skill work without Rarefriend?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires the Rarefriend MCP server to be running. It uses Rarefriend as the data layer for notes, tags, and enhanced search capabilities.

faq.question: Can I import contacts from a spreadsheet?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the bulk_create_contacts tool with up to 50 contacts per call. For larger lists, split them into sequential calls.

faq.question: How long does Google Contacts sync take?
faq.answer: Sync usually completes within a few minutes. Use get_integration_sync_status to check progress before searching synced contacts.

faq.question: What happens when the quota is exceeded?
faq.answer: The API returns a quota_exceeded response. Direct the user to rarefriend.com to upgrade their plan.

faq.question: Is my contact data shared with Rarefriend?
faq.answer: The skill uses OAuth to connect your Google Contacts to your Rarefriend account. Data is stored in your Rarefriend workspace under your own credentials.
